description: ORB Residential core 100 benchmark — synthetic only.

scenarios.title: Daily record after difficult school day
scenarios.input: Young person returned from school upset. Said lessons were difficult. Refused tea at first. Later settled after staff sat with them and offered a quiet space. Mood improved by evening.
scenarios.quality_focus: Daily record after difficult school day

scenarios.title: Positive progress daily record
scenarios.input: Young person had a calm day. Attended school on time. Joined group activity in the evening and shared positive news about a school project. Ate all meals. Bedtime routine completed without difficulty.

scenarios.title: Mealtime refusal
scenarios.input: Young person refused lunch saying they were not hungry. Staff offered alternative snack later. Young person ate a small portion at tea after calm conversation about food choices. No weight concern raised today.

scenarios.title: Sleep routine difficulty
scenarios.input: Young person struggled to settle at bedtime. Was awake until late. Staff offered quiet activity and reassurance. Eventually slept. Woke tired this morning. Sleep pattern to be monitored.

scenarios.title: Hygiene support
scenarios.input: Young person needed encouragement with personal care this morning. Staff offered sensitive support and choices. Young person completed hygiene routine with minimal prompting. Dressed appropriately for school.

scenarios.title: Peer relationship concern
scenarios.input: Young person said another young person in the home had been unkind during a game. Staff listened and facilitated a brief restorative conversation. Both young people agreed to try again tomorrow. No physical contact.

scenarios.title: Child requests private conversation
scenarios.input: Young person asked to speak privately before bedtime. Shared worries about school but did not want details recorded in front of others. Staff listened in private space and agreed to record proportionate summary. No disclosure of harm today.

scenarios.title: Child refuses activity
scenarios.input: Young person declined planned group outing. Said they needed quiet time. Staff respected choice and offered alternative activity at home. Young person engaged with one-to-one support instead.

scenarios.title: Child returns home unsettled
scenarios.input: Young person returned to the home after off-site appointment appearing unsettled. Would not say why immediately. Staff offered drink and quiet space. Presentation improved after 30 minutes. Follow-up keywork offered.

scenarios.title: Child has positive family contact
scenarios.input: Family contact visit at the home. Young person engaged well with family member. Shared news about school. Visit ended calmly. Young person said they enjoyed the visit. No concerns observed by staff.

scenarios.title: Property damage
scenarios.input: Young person became distressed in lounge. Threw cushion and damaged a picture frame. Staff used calm voice, offered space, no physical intervention. No injury. Manager informed by phone. Young person later apologised and helped tidy.

scenarios.title: Verbal aggression
scenarios.input: Young person shouted at staff during transition. Used raised voice but no threats of violence. Staff maintained low arousal and gave space. Young person calmed within ten minutes. No injury. Debrief offered.

scenarios.title: Door slamming / emotional distress
scenarios.input: Young person slammed bedroom door after disappointing phone call. Staff checked welfare through door. Young person said they needed space. Door opened later; young person accepted drink. No damage observed.

scenarios.title: Physical aggression without injury
scenarios.input: Young person pushed past staff during argument about screen time. No injury to staff or young person. Staff did not restrain. Manager notified. Young person later expressed regret. Risk review to be considered.

scenarios.title: Bullying concern
scenarios.input: Young person reported name-calling by a peer at school. Staff recorded words used. School to be informed. Young person did not want staff to call school today. Agreed to discuss with keyworker tomorrow.

scenarios.title: Threats to abscond
scenarios.input: Young person said they were going to leave the home and not return. Staff listened and maintained calm presence. Young person remained on site. Manager informed. Missing from care protocol discussed. No abscond occurred.

scenarios.title: Self-isolation after trigger
scenarios.input: After family phone call ended early, young person withdrew to bedroom. Declined dinner initially. Staff checked welfare hourly. Young person accepted snack later. Expressed feeling overwhelmed. Repair conversation planned.

scenarios.title: Conflict between young people
scenarios.input: Two young people argued over shared space. Raised voices but no physical contact. Staff separated and spoke with each individually. Both calmed. Agreed ground rules restated. Manager informed.

scenarios.title: Online conflict
scenarios.input: Young person upset after online messages from a peer. Showed staff the messages. No threats of violence. Staff supported young person to block contact and documented concerns. Online safety plan to be reviewed.

scenarios.title: Staff debrief after incident
scenarios.input: Team debrief after lounge incident. Staff reflected on de-escalation approach. Discussed what helped and what to do earlier next time. Agreed to update handover template. No disciplinary issues raised.

scenarios.title: Disclosure of feeling unsafe
scenarios.input: During keywork, young person said someone at a previous placement made them uncomfortable. Did not want to give names. Staff listened, did not press for detail, recorded words used. Escalated to manager/DSL same shift. No investigation by staff.

scenarios.title: Historic harm comment
scenarios.input: Young person mentioned historic harm in passing during daily routine. Did not want to elaborate. Staff recorded comment verbatim. Manager/DSL informed. No questioning beyond supportive listening.

scenarios.title: Concern about family contact
scenarios.input: Young person said they feel anxious before family contact but would not say why. Staff validated feelings. DSL informed. Contact plan to be reviewed at next professionals meeting. No allegation made.

scenarios.title: Online exploitation concern
scenarios.input: Young person received messages from unknown adult online offering gifts. Staff saw message content with young person's consent. DSL informed immediately. Device secured per policy. Chronology updated.

scenarios.title: Missing from care concern
scenarios.input: Young person left the home without permission. Returned two hours later. Appeared unsettled. Staff completed welfare check. Manager and social worker notified. Return home interview to be arranged.

scenarios.title: Substance concern
scenarios.input: Staff noticed unusual smell on young person's clothing. Young person denied substance use. Staff recorded observations without accusation. Manager informed. Health and safeguarding pathway to be followed per local policy.

scenarios.title: Allegation against adult
scenarios.input: Young person said an adult outside the home behaved inappropriately on a recent outing. Staff listened, recorded exact words, did not investigate. DSL and manager informed immediately. No contact with alleged adult by staff.

scenarios.title: Peer-on-peer concern
scenarios.input: Young person disclosed uncomfortable physical contact by another young person last week. Staff responded supportively. DSL informed. Separate supervision arranged. No questioning beyond supportive listening.

scenarios.title: Child returns with unexplained item
scenarios.input: Young person returned from off-site activity with expensive item they could not explain. Staff stored item securely. Recorded young person's account without pressure. Manager and social worker informed.

scenarios.title: Sexualised language / boundary concern
scenarios.input: Young person used sexualised language toward staff. Staff set boundary calmly and recorded incident. DSL informed. No punitive response. Context and pattern to be reviewed by manager.

scenarios.title: Distress after contact
scenarios.input: Young person tearful after family contact. Said goodbye was difficult. Staff offered quiet time and comfort. Young person calmed after 20 minutes. Agreed to talk in keywork session.

scenarios.title: School refusal
scenarios.input: Young person refused to leave for school. Said they felt sick. Staff explored worries calmly. Attended later after phone call with school. Pattern noted for education meeting.

scenarios.title: Sudden withdrawal
scenarios.input: Usually sociable young person withdrew to room all day. Minimal interaction. Staff checked in regularly. Young person said they needed space. No safeguarding disclosure. Monitor tomorrow.

scenarios.title: Anger after disappointment
scenarios.input: Young person angry when outing was cancelled due to weather. Raised voice and threw soft item. Staff validated disappointment. Young person regulated with support. Repair conversation completed.

scenarios.title: Repeated bedtime dysregulation
scenarios.input: Third night this week of bedtime dysregulation. Young person anxious about next day. Staff used consistent routine. Settled after extended reassurance. Pattern to be discussed in supervision.

scenarios.title: Food-related anxiety
scenarios.input: Young person anxious about group meal. Ate separately with staff support. Expressed worry about being judged. Staff validated and recorded. Dietician referral already in place.

scenarios.title: Refusal to attend appointment
scenarios.input: Young person refused CAMHS appointment. Said they were not ready. Staff respected choice and informed manager. Social worker to be updated. Alternative date to be offered.

scenarios.title: Avoiding key-work
scenarios.input: Young person cancelled keywork twice this week. Said sessions feel too formal. Staff offered informal check-in instead. Relationship repair planned with keyworker.

scenarios.title: Damaging bedroom item
scenarios.input: Young person damaged own bedroom item when upset. No injury. Staff supported regulation. Young person helped with tidy-up. Repair and replacement discussed calmly.

scenarios.title: Repair after conflict
scenarios.input: After earlier argument with staff, young person returned to apologise. Staff accepted repair. Brief conversation about triggers. Relationship restored. Positive end to shift.

scenarios.title: Rough daily note conversion
scenarios.input: rough notes: yp back 3ish upset school hard staff sat w them quiet rm better later tea refused first

scenarios.title: Rough incident note conversion
scenarios.input: rough: yp threw cushion frame broke no pi mgr phoned ok after

scenarios.title: Poor wording rewrite
scenarios.input: Young person kicked off at tea because they wanted attention. Staff told them to stop being dramatic. Later calmed down.

scenarios.title: Judgemental language rewrite
scenarios.input: Young person was manipulative and refused to comply. Staff had to be firm. Eventually did as told.

scenarios.title: Incomplete safeguarding note
scenarios.input: yp said something about old placement. told mgr.

scenarios.title: Incomplete handover
scenarios.input: school ok. mood low. meds? ask next shift.

scenarios.title: Voice-to-record correction
scenarios.input: voice transcript: um young person came back uh from school bit upset said lessons were hard um staff sat with them quiet room um better later

scenarios.title: Meeting note clean-up
scenarios.input: mtg: sw pep school home. att 78%. camhs wait. act: school mtg.

scenarios.title: Manager oversight rough notes
scenarios.input: reviewed incident. staff ok. debrief done. check transitions.

scenarios.title: Reg 44 evidence notes
scenarios.input: reg44 prep: stability ok. edu variable. 2 incidents de-esc only. sg chron updated. supervision current.

scenarios.title: Emotional check-in required
scenarios.input: Young person low mood after difficult phone call. Needs emotional check-in from next shift. Declined group activity. Accepted one-to-one support.

scenarios.title: Medication reminder
scenarios.input: Evening medication given at usual time. Morning dose due 08:00. Stock check needed — one week remaining. Young person compliant with medication.

scenarios.title: Family phone call follow-up
scenarios.input: Family phone call attempted — young person declined. Staff offered alternative time tomorrow. Young person said they need space first. No safeguarding concern from call attempt.

scenarios.title: School issue follow-up
scenarios.input: School reported concern about attendance pattern. Email received. Manager to call school tomorrow. Young person aware and anxious about call.

scenarios.title: Contact arrangements
scenarios.input: Family contact scheduled Saturday 14:00. Transport confirmed. Young person mixed feelings — excited and worried. Preparation time blocked in diary.

scenarios.title: Risk change update
scenarios.input: Risk assessment updated after incident. Missing from care risk increased to amber. Night checks increased. Manager briefed oncoming shift.

scenarios.title: Positive progress handover
scenarios.input: Calm shift. Young person engaged well with education and hobbies. Ate all meals. Positive phone call with social worker. Continue encouragement next shift.

scenarios.title: Health appointment handover
scenarios.input: GP appointment tomorrow 10:30. Young person nervous. Staff to accompany. Fasting not required. Bring red book equivalent care records.

scenarios.title: Sleep concern handover
scenarios.input: Young person slept only four hours. Tired and irritable this morning. May need flexible school start. Monitor presentation overnight.

scenarios.title: Peer dynamics handover
scenarios.input: Tension between two young people after shared activity dispute. Separated successfully. Both calmed. Avoid seating together at dinner until debrief.

scenarios.title: School motivation
scenarios.input: Keywork on school motivation. Young person said they struggle with maths. Identified one small goal for next week. Staff praised effort. Plan to involve school mentor.

scenarios.title: Family relationships
scenarios.input: Young person talked about complicated feelings toward family member. Staff listened without taking sides. Young person identified what helps before contact. No safeguarding disclosure.

scenarios.title: Identity and belonging
scenarios.input: Young person explored identity and belonging in keywork. Shared pride in cultural background. Staff validated experiences. Agreed to find resources for next session.

scenarios.title: Emotional regulation
scenarios.input: Keywork on emotional regulation strategies. Young person practised breathing exercise. Identified early warning signs. Staff reinforced strengths.

scenarios.title: Online safety
scenarios.input: Keywork on online safety. Reviewed privacy settings together. Young person asked about reporting concerning messages. Staff explained pathway to DSL.

scenarios.title: Healthy relationships
scenarios.input: Young person asked questions about healthy relationships. Staff used age-appropriate discussion. Boundaries and consent explored. No disclosure of harm.

scenarios.title: Preparing for review meeting
scenarios.input: Young person preparing for LAC review. Identified three wishes for meeting. Staff helped write notes. Young person nervous but willing to attend.

scenarios.title: Child unhappy with plan
scenarios.input: Young person said they disagree with contact plan. Staff recorded views accurately. Agreed to raise at review. Validated right to be heard.

scenarios.title: Restorative conversation
scenarios.input: Restorative conversation after peer conflict. Both young people heard each other's perspective with staff support. Agreed ground rules. Relationship improving.

scenarios.title: Building trust
scenarios.input: Young person new to home. Keywork focused on building trust. Explored likes and worries. Young person said staff feel approachable. Small goal set for week.

scenarios.title: Incident pattern review
scenarios.input: Manager reviewed three lounge incidents this month. Common trigger: transitions. Staff de-escalation appropriate. Actions: routine review, additional training.

scenarios.title: Family contact pattern
scenarios.input: Manager reviewed family contact pattern over six weeks. Young person fatigue after visits noted. Contact plan review requested with social worker.

scenarios.title: Missing episode oversight
scenarios.input: Manager oversight of missing episode. Return home interview completed. Chronology updated. Multi-agency notification confirmed. Missing plan to be revised.

scenarios.title: Safeguarding management note
scenarios.input: DSL oversight of partial disclosure case. Decision: chronology update and multi-agency discussion. No MASH on current information. Review in 48 hours.

scenarios.title: Staff practice reflection
scenarios.input: Manager reflected on staff recording quality after audit sample. Strengths in child voice. Development need: follow-up actions. Supervision plan updated.

scenarios.title: Placement plan review
scenarios.input: Placement plan review due. Education goals on track. Contact plan needs update. Health actions outstanding. Manager to chair internal review before statutory meeting.

scenarios.title: Risk assessment update
scenarios.input: Risk assessment updated after self-harm concern. Night checks increased. Sharp items stored securely. CAMHS contacted. Young person aware of safety plan.

scenarios.title: Complaints oversight
scenarios.input: Manager oversight of complaint about meal variety. Young person heard. Action plan agreed. Menu review with young people scheduled. Response within timescale.

scenarios.title: Positive progress oversight
scenarios.input: Manager noted sustained positive progress over six weeks. Improved school attendance. Reduced incidents. Commendation for staff team. Share at supervision.

scenarios.title: Reg 45 improvement note
scenarios.input: Reg 45 improvement action: handover quality. Progress: new template trialled. Evidence: audit sample improved. Next: train all shifts on template use.

scenarios.title: Care team meeting
scenarios.input: Internal care team meeting. Reviewed week events. Education, health, contact updates discussed. Actions assigned. Young person's views represented from keywork.

scenarios.title: Strategy discussion note
scenarios.input: Strategy discussion following partial disclosure about previous placement. DSL present. Decision: MASH referral not indicated on current information; chronology update and multi-agency meeting requested. Young person to be supported before any further questions. Review in 48 hours.

scenarios.title: Professionals meeting
scenarios.input: Education: attendance 78% this term; exclusions none. Social worker: care plan review due next month. CAMHS: waiting list; no appointment yet. Home: evenings calmer; mornings difficult. Actions: school meeting proposed; chronology shared with SW; CAMHS re-referral discussed.

scenarios.title: School meeting
scenarios.input: School meeting attended by home staff. Discussed attendance and behaviour support plan. School willing to provide mentor. Young person views shared with consent.

scenarios.title: LAC/review preparation
scenarios.input: Preparing for LAC review. Chronology extract prepared. Young person's wishes document completed. Health and education reports requested. Review date confirmed.

scenarios.title: Therapy feedback note
scenarios.input: Therapist feedback via phone. Young person engaged well in session. Homework strategies shared. Staff to reinforce coping skills daily. Next session in two weeks.

scenarios.title: Social worker update
scenarios.input: Phone update with social worker. Shared chronology summary. No new safeguarding concerns. Care plan review date confirmed. Social worker thanked team.

scenarios.title: Family contact review
scenarios.input: Last three contacts: first visit calm; second ended early when young person became tearful; third by phone — young person chose shorter call. Young person said visits feel rushed. Guardian requested longer weekend contact. Staff observed fatigue after visits.

scenarios.title: Health appointment summary
scenarios.input: GP appointment attended. Young person healthy. Sleep concerns discussed. Advice given on routine. Follow-up in three months unless concerns sooner.

scenarios.title: Police/professional contact note
scenarios.input: Police welfare check after missing episode. Young person safe. Officer spoke with manager. No arrest. Advice on missing plan documented. Chronology updated.

scenarios.title: Reg 44 evidence pack
scenarios.input: Preparing evidence for independent visitor: placement stability improving; education attendance variable; two incidents this month with de-escalation only; safeguarding chronology updated; staff supervision current; young person feedback collected via keywork.

scenarios.title: Reg 45 self-evaluation
scenarios.input: Reg 45 self-evaluation quarter. Strength: child voice in records. Development: handover consistency. Evidence samples attached. Action plan for next quarter drafted.

scenarios.title: Safer recruitment evidence reflection
scenarios.input: Manager reflected on safer recruitment file sample. All checks complete for recent hire. Induction documented. Supervision scheduled. Evidence ready for inspection.

scenarios.title: Staff supervision reflection
scenarios.input: Staff reflected on shift with two incidents and limited sleep. Felt rushed with records. Discussed de-escalation successes and need for earlier manager support on transitions. Agreed supervision focus on handover quality and earlier escalation when fatigued.

scenarios.title: Training impact note
scenarios.input: Team completed trauma-informed practice training. Staff reported increased confidence in de-escalation. Manager to observe practice and collect feedback next month.

scenarios.title: Quality of care review
scenarios.input: Monthly quality of care review. Sampled ten records. Strengths: safeguarding escalation timely. Development: outcome sections sometimes thin. Action: refresher on recording standards.

scenarios.title: Leadership oversight summary
scenarios.input: Registered Manager monthly oversight. Staffing stable. Two vacancies in recruitment. Incidents trending down. Reg 44 visit preparation on track. Children's views summary completed.

scenarios.title: Children's voice evidence
scenarios.input: Compiled children's voice evidence for review. Young person wishes: more say in activities, shorter contact when tired. Feedback collected via keywork and house meeting.

scenarios.title: Learning from incident review
scenarios.input: Learning review after property damage incident. Trigger: transition. Learning: earlier offer of quiet space. Action: update transition visual schedule. Shared in team meeting.

scenarios.title: Monthly manager summary
scenarios.input: Monthly manager summary: occupancy stable; education variable; safeguarding chronology current; supervision compliance 100%; two incidents with good de-escalation; contact plan review pending; Reg 44 visit next month.
